text:
TRANSTHORACIC ECHOCARDIOGRAM
PRE-CHEMOTHERAPY CARDIAC ASSESSMENT

INDICATION:
Ms. Renata Kowalczyk is a 48-year-old female with newly diagnosed acute myeloid leukemia (AML), FAB M4 subtype (acute myelomonocytic leukemia), presenting with leukocytosis (WBC 87,400/microliter), anemia (hemoglobin 7.2 g/dL), and thrombocytopenia at the time of diagnosis. She is being evaluated prior to initiation of induction chemotherapy with cytarabine and an anthracycline (daunorubicin)-based regimen plus FLT3 inhibitor (midostaurin). Past medical history is notable for type 2 diabetes mellitus managed with metformin and mild hypertension. This study is obtained to characterize baseline cardiac function and assess suitability for anthracycline-containing therapy.

SCAN TYPE:
Two-dimensional transthoracic echocardiography (2D TTE) with color flow Doppler, spectral Doppler (pulsed-wave and continuous-wave), and tissue Doppler imaging. No intravenous contrast agent administered. Study performed at rest in the left lateral decubitus position. Standard parasternal long-axis, parasternal short-axis, apical four-chamber, apical two-chamber, apical three-chamber (apical long-axis), and subcostal views obtained. Image quality: good to excellent throughout.

FINDINGS:

Left Ventricle:
The left ventricle is normal in size. Left ventricular internal diameter in diastole (LVIDd) measures 4.9 cm (normal range for female: up to 5.3 cm). Left ventricular internal diameter in systole (LVIDs) measures 3.1 cm. Left ventricular wall thickness is within normal limits: interventricular septum in diastole (IVSd) 0.9 cm, posterior wall in diastole (PWd) 0.9 cm; no evidence of concentric hypertrophy or asymmetric septal hypertrophy. Left ventricular systolic function is preserved and qualitatively normal on visual assessment. Biplane Simpson's method ejection fraction: 62% (lower limit of normal is 53% by ASE/EACVI criteria). No regional wall motion abnormalities identified in any of the standard coronary artery distributions (anterior, lateral, inferior, or septal walls). The left ventricular apex is well-visualized and appears normal without thrombus or apical aneurysm.

Left Ventricular Diastolic Function:
Diastolic filling appears normal. Mitral inflow pulsed-wave Doppler: E-wave velocity 72 cm/s, A-wave velocity 58 cm/s, E/A ratio 1.24 (normal). E-wave deceleration time 195 ms (normal). Lateral mitral annular e' velocity by tissue Doppler imaging: 10 cm/s (normal). Medial mitral annular e' velocity: 8 cm/s. E/e' ratio (lateral): 7.2 (below the threshold of concern for elevated filling pressures). Left atrial volume index estimated at 24 mL/m2 (upper limit of normal 34 mL/m2). Overall diastolic function: Grade I / normal diastolic function. No evidence of elevated left-sided filling pressures.

Left Atrium:
Left atrial size is normal. Left atrial anteroposterior dimension in parasternal long axis: 3.4 cm (normal). No spontaneous echo contrast or thrombus. No masses identified.

Right Ventricle:
The right ventricle is normal in size and wall thickness (RV free wall thickness < 5 mm). Right ventricular systolic function is visually normal. Tricuspid annular plane systolic excursion (TAPSE): 2.3 cm (normal, > 1.7 cm). No right ventricular wall motion abnormalities. The right ventricular outflow tract (RVOT) is unobstructed.

Right Atrium:
The right atrium is normal in size. No masses or thrombi.

Valvular Assessment:
Mitral valve: The mitral valve leaflets are thin, pliable, and move appropriately without restriction or prolapse. No mitral stenosis. Trivial physiologic mitral regurgitation noted on color flow Doppler; no hemodynamically significant mitral regurgitation.
Aortic valve: The aortic valve is tricuspid with thin, mobile leaflets. No calcification. No aortic stenosis (peak aortic valve velocity 1.3 m/s by CW Doppler, normal). No aortic regurgitation.
Tricuspid valve: Tricuspid valve morphology is normal. Trace physiologic tricuspid regurgitation present. Tricuspid regurgitation (TR) jet velocity 2.4 m/s, yielding an estimated right ventricular systolic pressure (RVSP) of approximately 28 mmHg (using modified Bernoulli equation, assuming right atrial pressure of 3 mmHg); this is consistent with normal pulmonary artery systolic pressure.
Pulmonic valve: Normal pulmonic valve. No pulmonic regurgitation of note.

Aorta:
Aortic root diameter at the sinuses of Valsalva: 3.0 cm (normal). Ascending aorta visualized in the proximal portion: normal caliber, 2.9 cm. No evidence of aortic root dilation.

Pericardium:
No pericardial effusion. No evidence of constrictive physiology. The pericardium appears thin and normal.

Inferior Vena Cava:
The inferior vena cava is normal in caliber (1.9 cm) with normal inspiratory collapse (>50%), suggesting normal central venous pressure.

Additional Observations:
Given the patient's known diagnosis of AML with markedly elevated white blood cell count, the study was reviewed for evidence of intracardiac leukostasis or mass lesion; none identified. No evidence of leukemic infiltration of the myocardium or pericardium. The study was technically limited to a small degree in the parasternal views due to body habitus, but apical windows were excellent and the overall diagnostic quality is sufficient for the clinical indication.

IMPRESSION:

1. Normal left ventricular size and systolic function. Left ventricular ejection fraction 62% by biplane Simpson's method, within the normal range. No regional wall motion abnormalities.

2. Normal left ventricular diastolic function with no evidence of elevated filling pressures. E/e' ratio 7.2, left atrial volume index normal.

3. Normal right ventricular size and function. TAPSE 2.3 cm. Estimated pulmonary artery systolic pressure 28 mmHg, within normal limits.

4. No hemodynamically significant valvular abnormalities. Trivial/trace physiologic regurgitation across mitral and tricuspid valves, as commonly encountered in the general population.

5. No pericardial effusion.

6. No intracardiac masses, thrombi, or structural abnormalities identified.

7. Normal aortic root dimensions.

Overall, this echocardiogram demonstrates preserved biventricular function with normal cardiac reserve. These findings are consistent with adequate cardiac suitability for planned anthracycline-based chemotherapy induction. Baseline LVEF documented at 62% for serial monitoring purposes given planned daunorubicin exposure.
